Functional Requirements (47 items (Avg Quality: 69/100)):
- ✅ 3.4 Security • User roles and uploaded documents shall be secured using role-based authentica- tion
- ✅ FR1: The system shall display extracted requirements and summaries through an interactive web dashboard
- ✅ FR3: The system shall generate visual summaries using Vision-Language Models (Qwen2.5-VL and LLaV A-13B)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall allow exporting results to external files (CSV , JSON)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall store structured requirement data in a SQLite database
- ✅ Project Requirements 2.3.1 Reliability • The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 120 hours during continuous operation
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall integrate with Trello and Jira for automated task creation
- ⚠️  The system shall ensure graceful degradation in case of OCR, VLM, or database errors
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall classify extracted information into Functional, Non-Functional, System, and Business requirements
- ⚠️  Users must manu- ally read documents and cre- ate tasks
- ⚠️  Sensitive configuration data must be stored in environment variables (.env)
- ⚠️  Each requirement category shall be color-coded for enhanced readability on the dashboard
- ❌ User created in SQLite DB with berypt hashed password User should be authenticated and token should be valid for 30 days As Expected, user created successfully [IMAGE IMG-6015733a52-32-1] OCR: Test Objective Precondition Steps Test data Expected result Post- condition Actual Result Pass/fail Verify PDF upload with image extraction and OCR processing User authenticated with valid JWT token
- ⚠️  Through its dashboard, project managers can visualize summaries, view extracted requirements, and export tasks directly to project management platforms, sig- nificantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy
- ✅ Extract using heuristic + semantic Document with requirements: - Functional ~ Non-functional ~ Business ~ System requirements Requirements categorized into 6 types
- ✅ 88 3.1 System Architecture Diagram of FlowMind (Source: Mid Report)
- ✅ 1 Architectural Design The architecture of FlowMind is based on a multi-tiered, modular design that separates concerns across major system components
- ⚠️  Include email, username, and password in request body 3
- ⚠️  Chroma: Open-source embedding database for llm applications
- ⚠️  System 2 Brief description of what Sys- tem 2 does and its primary features.Highlight the shortcomings or issues with System 2
- ⚠️  It summarizes the system context, overall functionality, major design decisions, and internal structure based on the FYP-1 Mid Report
- ⚠️  FlowMind improves upon these systems by integrating OCR, VLM-based understanding, semantic retrieval, automated classification, and task exporting into a single unified work- 2.3.1 Reliability
- ⚠️  System Overview 3.4.2 Class Diagram The class diagram from the Mid Report outlines key system classes such as DocumentU- ploader, DocumentParser, OCREngine, RequirementExtractor, and DashboardManager
- ⚠️  They ensure that the system ful- fills its intended purpose of automated document processing, requirement extraction, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Existing tools either provide basic text extraction, limited OCR capabilities, or manual task management, but none offer a fully integrated solution that understands both textual and visual content
- ⚠️  The requirements are derived from user interactions, system workflows, and expected operational behavior
- ⚠️  The system transforms unstructured documents into structured requirement sets categorized as Functional, Non-Functional, Business, and System-level requirements
- ⚠️  Key entities derived from the Mid Report include: •User: Represents clients, project managers, system administrators, and team mem- bers who interact with FlowMind
- ⚠️  Integration Layer: Supports automated task creation using Jira and Trello APIs, enabling seamless transition from extracted requirements to project management platforms
- ⚠️  Vision-Language Model processing for diagrams, charts, or visual workflows
- ⚠️  The system supports multiple document formats such as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG, and JPG, enabling users to upload and analyze diverse content
- ⚠️  The Iteration 2 activity flow includes upload, pars- ing, OCR, VLM summarization, embedding generation, requirement classification, and dashboard rendering
- ⚠️  The following layers collaborate to deliver the overall functionality: •Frontend Interface: A web application that enables users to upload documents, monitor progress, and view extracted summaries and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  The scope includes the following capabilities: • Automated parsing and preprocessing of uploaded documents
- ⚠️  It includes file upload, OCR, VLM processing, embedding retrieval, classification, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Although many platforms exist for document processing, project manage- ment, or OCR, very few offer an end-to-end automated pipeline for requirement extraction and classification
- ⚠️  FlowMind addresses these limitations by combining OCR, hybrid VLM processing, embedding- based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and automated requirement classification into a unified platform
- ⚠️  Project management platforms like Jira, Asana, and ClickUp allow task tracking but depend entirely on manual task creation
- ⚠️  FlowMind supports the following major interactions (as described in the Mid Report): • Upload and manage project-related documents

images.ocr:
Algorithm 1 Document Upload and Preprocessing

Input: File F uploaded by user
Output: Parsed text chunks and extracted images

1: Validate file format (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, JPG, PNG)
2: if (format = DOC or PPT) then

3: Convert using LibreOffice to DOCX/PPTX

4: end if

5: Extract raw text using file-type-specific parser

6: Extract embedded images (if any)

7: Store metadata in SQLite
images.summary:
• Algorithm 1 Document Upload and Preprocessing
• Input: File F uploaded by user
• Output: Parsed text chunks and extracted images
• 1: Validate file format (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, JPG, PNG)
• 2: if (format = DOC or PPT) then
• 3: Convert using LibreOffice to DOCX/PPTX

images.ocr:
Algorithm 2 OCR and Vision-Language Processing

Input: Extracted image |
Output: /mage-level understanding

1: Perform OCR using Tesseract to obtain text T

2: Pass image through VLM models (Qwen2.5-VL, LLaVA-13B)
3: Generate visual summary

4: Merge OCR text with VLM summary

Functional Requirements (47 items (Avg Quality: 69/100)):
- ✅ 3.4 Security • User roles and uploaded documents shall be secured using role-based authentica- tion
- ✅ FR1: The system shall display extracted requirements and summaries through an interactive web dashboard
- ✅ FR3: The system shall generate visual summaries using Vision-Language Models (Qwen2.5-VL and LLaV A-13B)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall allow exporting results to external files (CSV , JSON)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall store structured requirement data in a SQLite database
- ✅ Project Requirements 2.3.1 Reliability • The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 120 hours during continuous operation
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall integrate with Trello and Jira for automated task creation
- ⚠️  The system shall ensure graceful degradation in case of OCR, VLM, or database errors
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall classify extracted information into Functional, Non-Functional, System, and Business requirements
- ⚠️  Users must manu- ally read documents and cre- ate tasks
- ⚠️  Sensitive configuration data must be stored in environment variables (.env)
- ⚠️  Each requirement category shall be color-coded for enhanced readability on the dashboard
- ❌ User created in SQLite DB with berypt hashed password User should be authenticated and token should be valid for 30 days As Expected, user created successfully [IMAGE IMG-6015733a52-32-1] OCR: Test Objective Precondition Steps Test data Expected result Post- condition Actual Result Pass/fail Verify PDF upload with image extraction and OCR processing User authenticated with valid JWT token
- ⚠️  Through its dashboard, project managers can visualize summaries, view extracted requirements, and export tasks directly to project management platforms, sig- nificantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy
- ✅ Extract using heuristic + semantic Document with requirements: - Functional ~ Non-functional ~ Business ~ System requirements Requirements categorized into 6 types
- ✅ 88 3.1 System Architecture Diagram of FlowMind (Source: Mid Report)
- ✅ 1 Architectural Design The architecture of FlowMind is based on a multi-tiered, modular design that separates concerns across major system components
- ⚠️  Include email, username, and password in request body 3
- ⚠️  Chroma: Open-source embedding database for llm applications
- ⚠️  System 2 Brief description of what Sys- tem 2 does and its primary features.Highlight the shortcomings or issues with System 2
- ⚠️  It summarizes the system context, overall functionality, major design decisions, and internal structure based on the FYP-1 Mid Report
- ⚠️  FlowMind improves upon these systems by integrating OCR, VLM-based understanding, semantic retrieval, automated classification, and task exporting into a single unified work- 2.3.1 Reliability
- ⚠️  System Overview 3.4.2 Class Diagram The class diagram from the Mid Report outlines key system classes such as DocumentU- ploader, DocumentParser, OCREngine, RequirementExtractor, and DashboardManager
- ⚠️  They ensure that the system ful- fills its intended purpose of automated document processing, requirement extraction, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Existing tools either provide basic text extraction, limited OCR capabilities, or manual task management, but none offer a fully integrated solution that understands both textual and visual content
- ⚠️  The requirements are derived from user interactions, system workflows, and expected operational behavior
- ⚠️  The system transforms unstructured documents into structured requirement sets categorized as Functional, Non-Functional, Business, and System-level requirements
- ⚠️  Key entities derived from the Mid Report include: •User: Represents clients, project managers, system administrators, and team mem- bers who interact with FlowMind
- ⚠️  Integration Layer: Supports automated task creation using Jira and Trello APIs, enabling seamless transition from extracted requirements to project management platforms
- ⚠️  Vision-Language Model processing for diagrams, charts, or visual workflows
- ⚠️  The system supports multiple document formats such as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG, and JPG, enabling users to upload and analyze diverse content
- ⚠️  The Iteration 2 activity flow includes upload, pars- ing, OCR, VLM summarization, embedding generation, requirement classification, and dashboard rendering
- ⚠️  The following layers collaborate to deliver the overall functionality: •Frontend Interface: A web application that enables users to upload documents, monitor progress, and view extracted summaries and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  The scope includes the following capabilities: • Automated parsing and preprocessing of uploaded documents
- ⚠️  It includes file upload, OCR, VLM processing, embedding retrieval, classification, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Although many platforms exist for document processing, project manage- ment, or OCR, very few offer an end-to-end automated pipeline for requirement extraction and classification
- ⚠️  FlowMind addresses these limitations by combining OCR, hybrid VLM processing, embedding- based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and automated requirement classification into a unified platform
- ⚠️  Project management platforms like Jira, Asana, and ClickUp allow task tracking but depend entirely on manual task creation
- ⚠️  FlowMind supports the following major interactions (as described in the Mid Report): • Upload and manage project-related documents
- ⚠️  IBM DOORS A professional requirements management tool supporting traceability and versioning.Does not automatically ex- tract requirements from raw documents; requires exten- sive manual input

Functional Requirements (47 items (Avg Quality: 69/100)):
- ✅ 3.4 Security • User roles and uploaded documents shall be secured using role-based authentica- tion
- ✅ FR1: The system shall display extracted requirements and summaries through an interactive web dashboard
- ✅ FR3: The system shall generate visual summaries using Vision-Language Models (Qwen2.5-VL and LLaV A-13B)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall allow exporting results to external files (CSV , JSON)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall store structured requirement data in a SQLite database
- ✅ Project Requirements 2.3.1 Reliability • The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 120 hours during continuous operation
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall integrate with Trello and Jira for automated task creation
- ⚠️  The system shall ensure graceful degradation in case of OCR, VLM, or database errors
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall classify extracted information into Functional, Non-Functional, System, and Business requirements
- ⚠️  Users must manu- ally read documents and cre- ate tasks
- ⚠️  Sensitive configuration data must be stored in environment variables (.env)
- ⚠️  Each requirement category shall be color-coded for enhanced readability on the dashboard
- ❌ User created in SQLite DB with berypt hashed password User should be authenticated and token should be valid for 30 days As Expected, user created successfully [IMAGE IMG-6015733a52-32-1] OCR: Test Objective Precondition Steps Test data Expected result Post- condition Actual Result Pass/fail Verify PDF upload with image extraction and OCR processing User authenticated with valid JWT token
- ⚠️  Through its dashboard, project managers can visualize summaries, view extracted requirements, and export tasks directly to project management platforms, sig- nificantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy
- ✅ Extract using heuristic + semantic Document with requirements: - Functional ~ Non-functional ~ Business ~ System requirements Requirements categorized into 6 types
- ✅ 88 3.1 System Architecture Diagram of FlowMind (Source: Mid Report)
- ✅ 1 Architectural Design The architecture of FlowMind is based on a multi-tiered, modular design that separates concerns across major system components
- ⚠️  Include email, username, and password in request body 3
- ⚠️  Chroma: Open-source embedding database for llm applications
- ⚠️  System 2 Brief description of what Sys- tem 2 does and its primary features.Highlight the shortcomings or issues with System 2
- ⚠️  It summarizes the system context, overall functionality, major design decisions, and internal structure based on the FYP-1 Mid Report
- ⚠️  FlowMind improves upon these systems by integrating OCR, VLM-based understanding, semantic retrieval, automated classification, and task exporting into a single unified work- 2.3.1 Reliability
- ⚠️  System Overview 3.4.2 Class Diagram The class diagram from the Mid Report outlines key system classes such as DocumentU- ploader, DocumentParser, OCREngine, RequirementExtractor, and DashboardManager
- ⚠️  They ensure that the system ful- fills its intended purpose of automated document processing, requirement extraction, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Existing tools either provide basic text extraction, limited OCR capabilities, or manual task management, but none offer a fully integrated solution that understands both textual and visual content
- ⚠️  The requirements are derived from user interactions, system workflows, and expected operational behavior
- ⚠️  The system transforms unstructured documents into structured requirement sets categorized as Functional, Non-Functional, Business, and System-level requirements
- ⚠️  Key entities derived from the Mid Report include: •User: Represents clients, project managers, system administrators, and team mem- bers who interact with FlowMind
- ⚠️  Integration Layer: Supports automated task creation using Jira and Trello APIs, enabling seamless transition from extracted requirements to project management platforms
- ⚠️  Vision-Language Model processing for diagrams, charts, or visual workflows
- ⚠️  The system supports multiple document formats such as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG, and JPG, enabling users to upload and analyze diverse content
- ⚠️  The Iteration 2 activity flow includes upload, pars- ing, OCR, VLM summarization, embedding generation, requirement classification, and dashboard rendering
- ⚠️  The following layers collaborate to deliver the overall functionality: •Frontend Interface: A web application that enables users to upload documents, monitor progress, and view extracted summaries and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  The scope includes the following capabilities: • Automated parsing and preprocessing of uploaded documents
- ⚠️  It includes file upload, OCR, VLM processing, embedding retrieval, classification, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Although many platforms exist for document processing, project manage- ment, or OCR, very few offer an end-to-end automated pipeline for requirement extraction and classification
- ⚠️  FlowMind addresses these limitations by combining OCR, hybrid VLM processing, embedding- based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and automated requirement classification into a unified platform
- ⚠️  Project management platforms like Jira, Asana, and ClickUp allow task tracking but depend entirely on manual task creation
- ⚠️  FlowMind supports the following major interactions (as described in the Mid Report): • Upload and manage project-related documents
- ⚠️  IBM DOORS A professional requirements management tool supporting traceability and versioning.Does not automatically ex- tract requirements from raw documents; requires exten- sive manual input

Functional Requirements (49 items (Avg Quality: 69/100)):
- ✅ 3.4 Security • User roles and uploaded documents shall be secured using role-based authentica- tion
- ✅ FR1: The system shall display extracted requirements and summaries through an interactive web dashboard
- ✅ FR3: The system shall generate visual summaries using Vision-Language Models (Qwen2.5-VL and LLaV A-13B)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall allow exporting results to external files (CSV , JSON)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall store structured requirement data in a SQLite database
- ✅ Project Requirements 2.3.1 Reliability • The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 120 hours during continuous operation
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall integrate with Trello and Jira for automated task creation
- ⚠️  The system shall ensure graceful degradation in case of OCR, VLM, or database errors
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall classify extracted information into Functional, Non-Functional, System, and Business requirements
- ⚠️  The system shall display a visual progress indicator during file upload and process- ing
- ⚠️  Users must manu- ally read documents and cre- ate tasks
- ⚠️  Sensitive configuration data must be stored in environment variables (.env)
- ⚠️  Each requirement category shall be color-coded for enhanced readability on the dashboard
- 22 1 2: 3: Store embeddings in ChromaDB 4 5 : Apply RAG reasoning to classify each chunk into: : ¢ Functional Requirements 6: 7: ¢ Non-functional Requirements 8: « Business Requirements 9: : « System Requirements 10: Store all classified requirements in SQLite [IMAGE IMG-08b09cbaa3-30-2] OCR: Algorithm 4 Dashboard Rendering Input: Requirements dataset from SQLite Output: Interactive dashboard for user 1: Fetch categorized requirements 2: Generate summary metrics 3: Render UI components: 4: « Requirements list 5: ¢ Visual summaries 6: ¢ Export options [IMAGE IMG-809684d03d-31-1] OCR: Test Objective Precondition Test data Expected result Post- condition Actual Result Pass/fail Verify user signup with valid email and password User should not exist in database
- ⚠️  Furthermore, there is a lack of integrated systems that can analyze documents, classify requirements, generate insights, and auto- matically export tasks to project management tools such as Jira or Trello
- ⚠️  Through its dashboard, project managers can visualize summaries, view extracted requirements, and export tasks directly to project management platforms, sig- nificantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy
- ✅ 1 Architectural Design The architecture of FlowMind is based on a multi-tiered, modular design that separates concerns across major system components
- ⚠️  1 Algorithm Design The core algorithms of FlowMind revolve around three major operations: Document Pre- processing, Vision-Language Understanding, and Requirement Extraction using Retrieval- Augmented Generation
- ⚠️  Include email, username, and password in request body 3
- ⚠️  Chroma: Open-source embedding database for llm applications
- ⚠️  The following items are not included in the current scope: • Full collaboration features such as multi-user annotations
- ⚠️  System 2 Brief description of what Sys- tem 2 does and its primary features.Highlight the shortcomings or issues with System 2
- ⚠️  It summarizes the system context, overall functionality, major design decisions, and internal structure based on the FYP-1 Mid Report
- ⚠️  System Overview 3.4.2 Class Diagram The class diagram from the Mid Report outlines key system classes such as DocumentU- ploader, DocumentParser, OCREngine, RequirementExtractor, and DashboardManager
- ⚠️  They ensure that the system ful- fills its intended purpose of automated document processing, requirement extraction, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Existing tools either provide basic text extraction, limited OCR capabilities, or manual task management, but none offer a fully integrated solution that understands both textual and visual content
- ⚠️  The requirements are derived from user interactions, system workflows, and expected operational behavior
- ⚠️  4.4 Module 4: Dashboard and Integration This module presents results visually and connects the system to external project man- agement tools
- ⚠️  The system transforms unstructured documents into structured requirement sets categorized as Functional, Non-Functional, Business, and System-level requirements
- ⚠️  Key entities derived from the Mid Report include: •User: Represents clients, project managers, system administrators, and team mem- bers who interact with FlowMind
- ⚠️  Integration Layer: Supports automated task creation using Jira and Trello APIs, enabling seamless transition from extracted requirements to project management platforms
- ⚠️  The system uses a hybrid data- storage approach consisting of: •SQLite Database: Stores structured metadata, extracted textual summaries, and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  Vision-Language Model processing for diagrams, charts, or visual workflows
- ⚠️  Tra- ditional OCR tools such as Google Tesseract extract text from images or scanned PDFs but lack semantic understanding
- ⚠️  The system supports multiple document formats such as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG, and JPG, enabling users to upload and analyze diverse content
- ⚠️  The Iteration 2 activity flow includes upload, pars- ing, OCR, VLM summarization, embedding generation, requirement classification, and dashboard rendering
- ⚠️  It enables users to upload documents, extract both tex- tual and visual information, classify requirements, and visualize results on an interactive dashboard
- ⚠️  The following layers collaborate to deliver the overall functionality: •Frontend Interface: A web application that enables users to upload documents, monitor progress, and view extracted summaries and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  The scope includes the following capabilities: • Automated parsing and preprocessing of uploaded documents
- ⚠️  Although many platforms exist for document processing, project manage- ment, or OCR, very few offer an end-to-end automated pipeline for requirement extraction and classification
- ⚠️  Project management platforms like Jira, Asana, and ClickUp allow task tracking but depend entirely on manual task creation
- ⚠️  FlowMind supports the following major interactions (as described in the Mid Report): • Upload and manage project-related documents
- ⚠️  2 Problem Statement Organizations frequently receive lengthy and complex project documents that include mixed content such as text, diagrams, workflows, and embedded images
- ⚠️  IBM DOORS A professional requirements management tool supporting traceability and versioning.Does not automatically ex- tract requirements from raw documents; requires exten- sive manual input
- This significantly reduces the time, cost, and effort required for requirement analysis while improving ac- curacy, consistency, and scalability

Functional Requirements (49 items (Avg Quality: 69/100)):
- ✅ 3.4 Security • User roles and uploaded documents shall be secured using role-based authentica- tion
- ✅ FR1: The system shall display extracted requirements and summaries through an interactive web dashboard
- ✅ FR3: The system shall generate visual summaries using Vision-Language Models (Qwen2.5-VL and LLaV A-13B)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall allow exporting results to external files (CSV , JSON)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall store structured requirement data in a SQLite database
- ✅ Project Requirements 2.3.1 Reliability • The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 120 hours during continuous operation
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall integrate with Trello and Jira for automated task creation
- ⚠️  The system shall ensure graceful degradation in case of OCR, VLM, or database errors
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall classify extracted information into Functional, Non-Functional, System, and Business requirements
- ⚠️  The system shall display a visual progress indicator during file upload and process- ing
- ⚠️  Users must manu- ally read documents and cre- ate tasks
- ⚠️  Sensitive configuration data must be stored in environment variables (.env)
- ⚠️  Each requirement category shall be color-coded for enhanced readability on the dashboard
- 22 1 2: 3: Store embeddings in ChromaDB 4 5 : Apply RAG reasoning to classify each chunk into: : ¢ Functional Requirements 6: 7: ¢ Non-functional Requirements 8: « Business Requirements 9: : « System Requirements 10: Store all classified requirements in SQLite [IMAGE IMG-08b09cbaa3-30-2] OCR: Algorithm 4 Dashboard Rendering Input: Requirements dataset from SQLite Output: Interactive dashboard for user 1: Fetch categorized requirements 2: Generate summary metrics 3: Render UI components: 4: « Requirements list 5: ¢ Visual summaries 6: ¢ Export options [IMAGE IMG-809684d03d-31-1] OCR: Test Objective Precondition Test data Expected result Post- condition Actual Result Pass/fail Verify user signup with valid email and password User should not exist in database
- ⚠️  Furthermore, there is a lack of integrated systems that can analyze documents, classify requirements, generate insights, and auto- matically export tasks to project management tools such as Jira or Trello
- ⚠️  Through its dashboard, project managers can visualize summaries, view extracted requirements, and export tasks directly to project management platforms, sig- nificantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy
- ✅ 1 Architectural Design The architecture of FlowMind is based on a multi-tiered, modular design that separates concerns across major system components
- ⚠️  1 Algorithm Design The core algorithms of FlowMind revolve around three major operations: Document Pre- processing, Vision-Language Understanding, and Requirement Extraction using Retrieval- Augmented Generation
- ⚠️  Include email, username, and password in request body 3
- ⚠️  Chroma: Open-source embedding database for llm applications
- ⚠️  The following items are not included in the current scope: • Full collaboration features such as multi-user annotations
- ⚠️  System 2 Brief description of what Sys- tem 2 does and its primary features.Highlight the shortcomings or issues with System 2
- ⚠️  It summarizes the system context, overall functionality, major design decisions, and internal structure based on the FYP-1 Mid Report
- ⚠️  System Overview 3.4.2 Class Diagram The class diagram from the Mid Report outlines key system classes such as DocumentU- ploader, DocumentParser, OCREngine, RequirementExtractor, and DashboardManager
- ⚠️  They ensure that the system ful- fills its intended purpose of automated document processing, requirement extraction, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Existing tools either provide basic text extraction, limited OCR capabilities, or manual task management, but none offer a fully integrated solution that understands both textual and visual content
- ⚠️  The requirements are derived from user interactions, system workflows, and expected operational behavior
- ⚠️  4.4 Module 4: Dashboard and Integration This module presents results visually and connects the system to external project man- agement tools
- ⚠️  The system transforms unstructured documents into structured requirement sets categorized as Functional, Non-Functional, Business, and System-level requirements
- ⚠️  Key entities derived from the Mid Report include: •User: Represents clients, project managers, system administrators, and team mem- bers who interact with FlowMind
- ⚠️  Integration Layer: Supports automated task creation using Jira and Trello APIs, enabling seamless transition from extracted requirements to project management platforms
- ⚠️  The system uses a hybrid data- storage approach consisting of: •SQLite Database: Stores structured metadata, extracted textual summaries, and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  Vision-Language Model processing for diagrams, charts, or visual workflows
- ⚠️  Tra- ditional OCR tools such as Google Tesseract extract text from images or scanned PDFs but lack semantic understanding
- ⚠️  The system supports multiple document formats such as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, PNG, and JPG, enabling users to upload and analyze diverse content
- ⚠️  The Iteration 2 activity flow includes upload, pars- ing, OCR, VLM summarization, embedding generation, requirement classification, and dashboard rendering
- ⚠️  It enables users to upload documents, extract both tex- tual and visual information, classify requirements, and visualize results on an interactive dashboard
- ⚠️  The following layers collaborate to deliver the overall functionality: •Frontend Interface: A web application that enables users to upload documents, monitor progress, and view extracted summaries and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  The scope includes the following capabilities: • Automated parsing and preprocessing of uploaded documents
- ⚠️  Although many platforms exist for document processing, project manage- ment, or OCR, very few offer an end-to-end automated pipeline for requirement extraction and classification
- ⚠️  Project management platforms like Jira, Asana, and ClickUp allow task tracking but depend entirely on manual task creation
- ⚠️  FlowMind supports the following major interactions (as described in the Mid Report): • Upload and manage project-related documents
- ⚠️  2 Problem Statement Organizations frequently receive lengthy and complex project documents that include mixed content such as text, diagrams, workflows, and embedded images
- ⚠️  IBM DOORS A professional requirements management tool supporting traceability and versioning.Does not automatically ex- tract requirements from raw documents; requires exten- sive manual input
- This significantly reduces the time, cost, and effort required for requirement analysis while improving ac- curacy, consistency, and scalability

Functional Requirements (26 items (Avg Quality: 67/100)):
- ✅ The system shall provide a self-registration process for job seekers
- ✅ The system shall support account activation by user mobile number FR-03
- ✅ The system shall offer the CV to AI engine to automatically extract information from uploaded resumes/CVs using AI parsing technology and add the extracted information to the profile
- ⚠️  The system shall support resume/CV upload in common formats (PDF, DOCX, TXT)
- ⚠️  The system shall allow the job seeker to upload supplementary documents (i.e
- ⚠️  The system shall generate a shareable public profile URL/QR Code for each job seeker, if activated by the user
- ⚠️  The system shall provide a recommendation to encourage job seekers to and the Palestinian Ministry of Labour (MoL)
- ⚠️  The system shall provide privacy controls that allow job seekers to set visibility preferences for their profile (public or private), and request account deactivation or deletion
- ⚠️  Document Conventions This document follows these conventions: Term Description shall Refers to a mandatory requirement that must be fulfilled during Phase 1 of platform development
- ⚠️  The system shall allow job seekers to edit and update their profiles at any time
- ⚠️  The system shall allow job seekers to set job preferences including job type, industry, location, salary expectations, and work arrangements
- ⚠️  The system shall provide a stepwise registration process for employers:  Level 1: Company name, email, mobile number, company ID, and registration number (if registered)  Level 2: Website, industry, company size, address, company description, and attachments for supplementary documents FR-14
- Design and Implementation Constraints The following constraints will impact the design and implementation of the platform: 1) Technical Constraints  must be able to integrate with existing MoL and PEF systems and databases  must support integration with other external job sites 3.6.1
- The system shall provide a recommendation to encourage job seekers to complete their profiles and to explore opportunities aligned with their broader skills, interests, and experiences beyond formal education and training
- ⚠️  should Indicates a requirement that will take place in phase 2 and the developer should take it into consideration for future scalability
- ✅ It will also form the basis for the Terms of Reference (ToR) for the Phase Two procurement process for system implementation
- ⚠️  Product Perspective The AI-Driven Job Matching Platform is a new system being developed to address the challenges of labor market efficiency in Palestine
- ⚠️  Assumptions and Dependencies Assumptions 1) MoL and PEF will provide timely access to existing systems and databases for integration 5.7
- ⚠️  System Features and Functional Requirements
- ⚠️  TBD To Be Determined, indicates information that is not yet available but will be provided in future versions Note Provides additional information or clarification Requirements are categorized as follows: Requirement Number Description FR-XX Functional Requirements NFR-XX Non-Functional Requirements IR-XX Interface Requirements DR-XX Data Requirements SR-XX Security Requirements 1.3
- In Scope The platform will include the following key components:  User Management System: Registration and profile management for job seekers, employers, external job sites, and administrators  Job Posting Management System: Creation, publishing, and search for job opportunities  AI-Driven Matching Engine: Skill-based matching algorithms using natural language processing, and recommender systems  Integration Framework: APIs and adapters for connecting with external job sites and government databases  Reporting and Analytics: Dashboards and reports for monitoring system performance and labor market trends Including (jobs posted, and users registered etc.)  Security Framework: Authentication, authorization, and data protection mechanisms  Multilingual Support: Full functionality in both Arabic and English 5.4.1
- Out of Scope The following items are explicitly excluded from this phase of the project: 1) Implementation of the full Labor Market Information System (LMIS) 2) Hardware procurement for hosting the solution 3) Integration with systems beyond those specified in this document 4) Training of end-users (this will be addressed in Phase Two) 5) Long-term support and maintenance (to be addressed in separate agreements) 6) Mobile application development 8 1.5
- ❌ Purpose This Software Requirements Specification (SRS) document provides a comprehensive description of the AI-Driven Job Matching Platform to be developed for the Ministry of Labor (MoL) and Palestinian Employment Fund (PEF) in Palestine
- ❌ The platform will interface with:  Existing MoL and PEF databases and systems  External job sites selected by MoL and PEF  Government databases for verification and data enrichment  Educational institutions for skills and qualification validation The following context diagram illustrates the system's position within its environment and its interactions with external entities: 2.2
- ❌ System Performance Metrics
- ❌ The document details the functional and non- functional requirements for the platform, which aims to enhance labor market efficiency by providing real-time data integration, standardizing job postings, and facilitating employment for job seekers

Non-Functional Requirements (3 items (Avg Quality: 46/100)):
- If automatic verification fails, the Ministry of Labor (MoL) shall be able to 1) Technical Constraints  must be able to integrate with existing MoL and PEF systems and databases  must support integration with other external job sites  must be accessible via standard web browsers without special plugins or settings  must function effectively in areas with limited internet connectivity  must be user-friendly especially with people with disability 2) Regulatory Constraints  must comply with Palestinian data privacy and protection regulations  must adhere to international standards for data security (e.g., GDPR principles)  must support legal requirements for employment and recruitment 3) Business Constraints  must be implemented in phases, with basic functionality prioritized  must be scalable to accommodate future expansion  must be maintainable by MoL technical staff with appropriate training 4) User Constraints  must accommodate users with varying levels of technical proficiency 2) Employers  Private sector enterprises of various sizes  Different industry sectors and geographical locations  Varying levels of technical sophistication  Primary needs: Posting job opportunities, finding qualified candidates, managing recruitment process 3) External Job Sites  Existing job portals and recruitment platforms  Varying technical capabilities and data structures  Primary needs: Data exchange, expanded reach, standardized job posting format 4) System Administrators  MoL and PEF staff responsible for platform management  Technical personnel with system administration skills  Primary needs: System monitoring, user management, data management, reporting 5) Government Officials  Decision-makers at MoL, PEF, and other government entities  Policy makers and labor market analysts  Primary needs: Labor market insights, employment statistics, system performance metrics 2.4
- ❌ Employer Registration and Profile Management ID Requirement  Security Framework: Authentication, authorization, and data protection mechanisms  Multilingual Support: Full functionality in both Arabic and English  Limited onboarding and testing sessions with internal stakeholders
- ❌ 19  User activity monitoring and reporting  Employment statistics and trends  System performance metrics  Custom report generation 6) System Administration  User management and access control  System configuration and customization  Data management and backup  Security monitoring and audit  System performance management and control 2.3

User Requirements / Stories (4 items (Avg Quality: 66/100)):
- ⚠️  The system shall provide an employer verification process that includes automatic verification using official information such as the employer’s registration number, VAT number, and registered mobile number as recorded in government databases
- ⚠️  It will operate as a standalone web-based system initially but is designed to become the core component of a more comprehensive Labor Market Information System (LMIS) in the future
- 17 This SRS will serve as the foundation for the subsequent system design and development phases, ensuring that all stakeholders have a clear understanding of what the system will do and how it will operate
- The platform will serve as the core component of the future Labor Market Information System (LMIS), integrating multiple job sources and government databases to optimize job matching, provide analytics, and support decision-making

Business Requirements (4 items (Avg Quality: 60/100)):
- ⚠️  The system shall collect the following information from job seekers through a multi-level onboarding process (via mobile/email):  Level 1: First and last name, email, mobile number, password, gender  Level 2: Education, work experience, skills, training, certificates, recent salary (optional), current address, permanent address/residence  Level 3 (optional) : Social media links (e.g., LinkedIn, IEEE, X), personal statement, bio  Policy makers and labor market analysts  Primary needs: Labor market insights, employment statistics, system performance metrics 2.4
- ⚠️  50 5 Glossary Term Definition AI Artificial Intelligence API Application Programming Interface GDPR General Data Protection Regulation LMIS Labor Market Information System MoL Ministry of Labor Enabel Belgian agency for international cooperation NLP Natural Language Processing PEF Palestinian Employment Fund RBAC Role-Based Access Control RTO Recovery Time Objective RPO Recovery Point Objective SLA Service Level Agreement SRS System Requirements Specification ToR Terms of Reference WCAG Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.1
- Intended Audience This document is intended for the following stakeholders: Stakeholder Role Ministry of Labor (MoL) Officials who will review and approve the requirements and solution owner
- ❌ 35 1) Terms of Reference "Development of System Requirements Specification (SRS) for a Job Matching Platform" (Reference #: PSE21002-10053) 2) National Employment Strategic Plan (2021-2025) 3) Youth Economic Empowerment in Palestine (YEP) program documentation 4) Existing analysis, assessments, and AS-IS case studies provided by MoL ,PEF and Enabel team 5) Research on best practices in AI-driven job matching platforms 6) Meetings with stakeholders as identified by the consultancy ToR 2

- ✅ 3.4 Security • User roles and uploaded documents shall be secured using role-based authentica- tion
- ✅ FR1: The system shall display extracted requirements and summaries through an interactive web dashboard
- ✅ FR3: The system shall generate visual summaries using Vision-Language Models (Qwen2.5-VL and LLaV A-13B)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall allow exporting results to external files (CSV , JSON)
- ✅ FR3: The system shall store structured requirement data in a SQLite database
- ✅ Project Requirements 2.3.1 Reliability • The system shall have a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 120 hours during continuous operation
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall integrate with Trello and Jira for automated task creation
- ⚠️  The system shall ensure graceful degradation in case of OCR, VLM, or database errors
- ⚠️  FR2: The system shall classify extracted information into Functional, Non-Functional, System, and Business requirements
- ⚠️  The system shall display a visual progress indicator during file upload and process- ing
- ⚠️  Users must manu- ally read documents and cre- ate tasks
- ⚠️  Sensitive configuration data must be stored in environment variables (.env)
- ⚠️  Each requirement category shall be color-coded for enhanced readability on the dashboard

- ⚠️  Furthermore, there is a lack of integrated systems that can analyze documents, classify requirements, generate insights, and auto- matically export tasks to project management tools such as Jira or Trello
- ⚠️  Through its dashboard, project managers can visualize summaries, view extracted requirements, and export tasks directly to project management platforms, sig- nificantly reducing manual effort and improving accuracy
- ✅ 1 Architectural Design The architecture of FlowMind is based on a multi-tiered, modular design that separates concerns across major system components
- ⚠️  1 Algorithm Design The core algorithms of FlowMind revolve around three major operations: Document Pre- processing, Vision-Language Understanding, and Requirement Extraction using Retrieval- Augmented Generation

- ⚠️  They ensure that the system ful- fills its intended purpose of automated document processing, requirement extraction, and dashboard visualization
- ⚠️  Existing tools either provide basic text extraction, limited OCR capabilities, or manual task management, but none offer a fully integrated solution that understands both textual and visual content
- ⚠️  The requirements are derived from user interactions, system workflows, and expected operational behavior
- ⚠️  4.4 Module 4: Dashboard and Integration This module presents results visually and connects the system to external project man- agement tools
- ⚠️  The system transforms unstructured documents into structured requirement sets categorized as Functional, Non-Functional, Business, and System-level requirements
- ⚠️  Key entities derived from the Mid Report include: •User: Represents clients, project managers, system administrators, and team mem- bers who interact with FlowMind
- ⚠️  Integration Layer: Supports automated task creation using Jira and Trello APIs, enabling seamless transition from extracted requirements to project management platforms

- ⚠️  The Iteration 2 activity flow includes upload, pars- ing, OCR, VLM summarization, embedding generation, requirement classification, and dashboard rendering
- ⚠️  It enables users to upload documents, extract both tex- tual and visual information, classify requirements, and visualize results on an interactive dashboard
- ⚠️  The following layers collaborate to deliver the overall functionality: •Frontend Interface: A web application that enables users to upload documents, monitor progress, and view extracted summaries and requirement classifications
- ⚠️  The scope includes the following capabilities: • Automated parsing and preprocessing of uploaded documents
- ⚠️  Although many platforms exist for document processing, project manage- ment, or OCR, very few offer an end-to-end automated pipeline for requirement extraction and classification
- ⚠️  Project management platforms like Jira, Asana, and ClickUp allow task tracking but depend entirely on manual task creation
- ⚠️  FlowMind supports the following major interactions (as described in the Mid Report): • Upload and manage project-related documents

- ❌ Options to be determined (on-premises, cloud, or hybrid) 2) Hardware Environment  Server infrastructure capable of supporting AI processing requirements  Adequate storage for user data, job listings, and analytics  Network infrastructure with sufficient bandwidth, high-availability, load balancing, auto scaling and reliability  Disaster recovery capabilities 11 3) Software Environment  Modern web technologies and frameworks  Database management system with high performance and scalability RTO Recovery Time Objective RPO Recovery Point Objective SLA Service Level Agreement SRS System Requirements Specification ToR Terms of Reference WCAG Web Content Accessibility Guidelines YEP Youth Economic Empowerment in Palestine 6 1.
- ❌ Features and Functional Requirements.
